## [00:00 - 03:03]

So this lesson's about launch metrics, the numbers that you generate in your launch, and you're going to generate a lot of numbers, a lot of data, that's just the nature of a launch. And even if you're not a math person, I think you're gonna like some of these numbers, especially the number stores the end when it comes down to sales. So I just wanted to cover some of the key things for you to think about in your launch. Now, one caveat here, all these numbers are just examples. They're just examples, depending on your market, your list, the source of traffic, the technology you're using, your offer, your pricing, on and on, they'll vary all over the place. And I made some of these numbers super just nice and round to make the math easy, okay? So first of all, one of the biggest numbers that you're gonna wanna watch is the opt-in rate. That's the opt-in rate. So this is simply of the people that get to your opt-in page or your squeeze page, how many of them opt-in and join your list? This is a key number in any online business. It's a super, it's an important number for you to watch at any time, but in a launch, it's one of the first pieces of data you're gonna get. So first of all, your opt-in rate varies dramatically, dramatically by your traffic source. So there's, when I think about this, so this is your squeeze page right here, okay? That's your squeeze page. There's your squeeze page. And you could have traffic coming from your list. So sometimes when we have an existing list and we wanna do a launch, we'll actually send people into an opt-in to get them to opt-in to our launch list. And so you can send, and if it's your list and your traffic and they already know you, then you will typically see a very high opt-in rate. And it could be anywhere from 40 to 70%. I've even seen higher than 70%. If you're down below 40%, unless it's a really different offer, unless if people join your list for something and it's really, really different kind of offer for almost a different market, you might see a lower rate, but you should generally see a very, very high rate. The next one would be joint venture partners or affiliates. This is endorsed traffic. And for this, I'd be looking at maybe a 30 to 40%. I've seen up to 50%, even like as high as 55%, but if it gets below 30%, I'm starting to get a little grumpy. And then we have what would be called cold traffic or paid traffic. This is where you're running ads maybe on Facebook or somewhere else you're running ads. And now this traffic will opt-in at a much, much lower rate,

## [03:03 - 06:06]

probably less than 5% for just purely cold traffic. That's never heard of you before. And now you're popping up an ad in front of them. Probably gonna be less than 5%. These people already know you and love you or you're endorsed traffic, endorsed mean these partners said, hey, go check out Jeff's launch. He's really awesome and he's got great stuff to share. 30, 40, 50% cold traffic, much, much less. So let's just pretend we're using either JVs or your list and we get 1,000 people to your squeeze page. And then of those 1,000 people, we had 400 opt-in to your list. So the math here is pretty easy. It's just 4,000 or 400 divided by 1,000 equals a 40% opt-in rate. That's this number. So here I was just sort of giving you these numbers, these ranges, I just did some math for you. We had 1,000 people come here. We had 400 join. We've got a 40% opt-in rate. Okay, so now you've got your list right here. I better change colors here because this is gonna be complicated. Now, you've seen this, right? You've seen this. You know what this is. Now, the next thing you need to look at is, and this is where the numbers get really crazy or really hard to interpret, is your open rate and your click rate. So this might be a surprise to some people when they first start building a list, but not everyone who joins your list is actually going to open the emails you send. In fact, the majority of them probably won't open your email. So here's the thing that you have to understand about open rates and click rates, is that first of all, we have this very important thing. I'm gonna put this in red because it's so important. We have this thing we call recency. And what that means is that the people that have joined your list recently will be more active. They'll open more and they'll click more. And I know I do one big launch a year, one big joint venture launch a year. And I know that when people join that list, they might be opening at 20 to even 40%. But if I look at my list that is a year old, they're probably opening at about a 10% rate. And if I look at my list that's two years old,

## [06:06 - 09:08]

it might be 5%. And if I look at my list that's three years old, it might be 2% and the really old one might be 1%. And it's just because the recency, these people joined the list more recently. So when you hear people talking about, oh, my click rate or my open rate or open rates are falling or click rates are falling or it's hard to get people to open, and then someone will say, well, what's your open rate? Well, the idea of answering that question, it's just a ludicrous question because it completely depends on a whole bunch of factors. One of the factors that depends on is what traffic source it came from. Another one is what they opted in for on this page. Another one is all the emails you sent, but a big one is just how recently they joined your list. So you can't compare one list's open rate or one business's open rate or you can't compare list to list, you can't compare time to time. You can only compare to your recent past. Like how did this email compare to the email I sent last week or that I sent two weeks ago? Also, technology impacts this. Different people's email, sort of email programs will generate different click rates. Different email service providers will show different click rates even though the same number of people clicked. So the numbers are all over the place. But one thing I can tell you is that when you're in a launch and you start sending into these, your open rate and your click rate will generally fall throughout the launch. That's just the reality. People will open here, those first few emails you send them more than they'll open as you go into the launch and especially as you go into your sales video, your open cart, click rates and open rates will drop as you go through the sequence. That's just the reality. So typically, I mean on a launch list that where I'm getting from, where the traffic is from joint venture partners, endorsed traffic, I might see my click rate might be, my open rate might be 15 to 30%, my click rate might be five to 15%. ## [12:10 - 14:14]

and this one generated $50 a lead. Now obviously conversions, all these numbers can vary all over the place, but what I like to be able to do is I like to be able, if say this is an internal launch and now you wanted to roll out to join venture partners, when you know these numbers, then you can go back to them and say, I generated $50 a lead. When I did this as an internal launch, I generated $50 a lead and so it converts really, really well. So if you send me traffic, then I'm going to be able to pay you a lot of commissions. If you're paying a 40% commission, that would be a little bit less, but you can do all this math is pretty simple, even though I'm sure it looks complicated with this diagram. These are the things you need to watch. Opt-in rate, open rate, click rate, conversion rate, and dollars per lead. All these, my favorite too would be the opt-in rate and the dollars per lead. So like I said, you're going to get a lot of data. You're going to generate a lot of math. These numbers are all going to vary wildly by market, by offer, by the list, by the traffic source, frankly by how you put your launch together. But you want to track these. I would track them all in a spreadsheet. Just Google Docs has got, it's free, and you can create a spreadsheet that tracks all of this and track them from launch to launch, because this isn't going to be your last launch. And these numbers are great for attracting joint venture partners. So make sure you have a way, make sure you're tracking these during your launch so you can make adjustments during your launch and ahead of your next launch. These numbers are important.
